$meta.title: Agentic HRMS — Machine-Readable Architecture Map
$meta.purpose: Single source of truth describing the ACTUAL code of this repo (backend FastAPI + SQLAlchemy + Alembic + LangGraph agents; frontend React + Vite + TS). Built for pasting into chat-based AI tools (ChatGPT / DeepSeek) so they can debug, extend, and modify the code correctly without opening the repo.
$meta.generated_from: static read of the source on the commit below — no assumptions; verified facts only
$meta.all_paths_are: relative to repo root unless marked abs
how_to_use_with_ai: Paste this whole file (or, for smaller-context tools, paste only the relevant top-level section named by docs/architecture.index.md) as context before asking the tool to change code.
Tell the tool: 'Only touch the files named in the relevant section. Match the existing pattern shown. Cite file:line in your answer.'
For any change, first read the matching entry under `playbooks` — it lists the exact files and the existing function/pattern to copy.
After the tool produces a code change, run the matching `update_protocol` step so this doc stays in sync.
conventions_for_ai: Never invent file paths, table names, columns, routes, or permission codes — every one that exists is listed here. If something is not here, say so instead of guessing.
Backend Python: SQLAlchemy 2.0 typed models (Mapped[...] / mapped_column), Pydantic v2 (model_config, from_attributes=True). Windows dev box; run backend commands through backend/.venv.
Frontend: TypeScript strict, path alias '@' -> Frontend/src, no axios (custom fetch client in services/api.ts), TanStack Query for server state, Zustand for auth/UI state.
Mutations are RBAC-gated on the backend via require_permissions(...) AND hidden on the frontend via hasPermission(...) — change BOTH when adding a gated feature.
Soft delete everywhere: set deleted_at, never hard-delete; all list queries filter deleted_at IS NULL.
Most agent request/response Pydantic schemas live INSIDE each agent package (app/agents/<x>/schemas.py), NOT in app/schemas/.

prerequisites.postgres: 16 running locally; database named 'hrms' must exist before migrating

backend_setup: python -m scripts.seed_auth   (NOT `python scripts/seed_auth.py` — that raises ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'app')
frontend_setup: copy .env.example .env  (set VITE_API_BASE_URL to match the backend, e.g. http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/v1)

backend_.env.file: backend/.env (gitignored; template backend/.env.example). Loaded by app/core/config.py Settings(env_file='.env').

keys.ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES: default 60 (note: config field access_token_expire_minutes has no env alias — see known_issues)
keys.OPENAI_API_KEY: enables the LLM intent classifier; if absent the system falls back to rule-based intent

keys.VITE_API_BASE_URL: backend API base incl. /api/v1. Code fallback if unset is http://127.0.0.1:8001/api/v1 (see known_issues — the :8001 fallback mismatches the documented :8000 backend).

db.migrate: cd backend && alembic upgrade head   (alembic env.py pulls the URL from settings.database_url, overriding alembic.ini)
db.admin_credentials: from .env ADMIN_EMAIL / ADMIN_PASSWORD (example: admin@example.com / ChangeMe123!) — superuser with all permissions
db.reset_hint: drop & recreate the DB then `alembic upgrade head` again; there is no down-to-base convenience script

smoke_scripts.backend/scripts/smoke_agent_command.py: TestClient: login, POST /agent-command/send x3, list workflows
smoke_scripts.backend/scripts/smoke_agent_runtime.py: TestClient: login, GET /agents/registry, POST /agents/command, fetch workflow events
smoke_scripts.backend/scripts/smoke_approval_engine.py: TestClient: create approval, list pending, approve, resume-workflow
one-off data-repair utility (backfills names, strips generated identifiers, dedups employees)

stacks.backend: FastAPI 0.115, SQLAlchemy 2.0, Alembic 1.15, psycopg 3, Pydantic v2 / pydantic-settings, python-jose (JWT), bcrypt, LangGraph 0.4 + LangChain 0.3 + langchain-openai, pdfplumber + python-docx (resume parsing)
stacks.frontend: React 19, Vite 6, TypeScript 5.8, react-router-dom 6, Zustand 5, TanStack Query 5 + Table 8, Tailwind 3.4, framer-motion, lucide-react

architecture_overview.request_flow: HTTP -> CORSMiddleware -> AuthContextMiddleware (best-effort: sets request.state.user_id from a valid access token, never rejects) -> api_router mounted at /api/v1 -> endpoint. Endpoints that need identity/permissions use FastAPI Depends: get_current_user / require_permissions from app/api/deps.py (these DO reject with 401/403).
architecture_overview.agent_command_flow: Frontend AgentCommandPage -> POST /api/v1/agent-command/send -> coordinator_agent/command_api.py -> CoordinatorRuntimeService.submit_command (coordinator_agent/service.py): (1) ExecutionTracker.start_run creates an AgentRun; (2) natural_language_extractor.extract(command) => IntentExtraction (rules + optional OpenAI); (3) compute fallback route (_analyze_intent keyword router) and extraction route (_route_from_extraction); (4) if missing_fields OR confidence<INTENT_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD => clarify; (5) route precedence: explicit 'revise salary' > extraction route > fallback route; (6) _invoke_domain_agent calls the domain agent's .execute(); (7) if the action is a CRITICAL_ACTION or the agent returns approval_request_id => create ApprovalRequest and set workflow WAITING_APPROVAL. Workflow state is serialized onto AgentRun.metadata_json keyed by AgentRun.correlation_id (== public workflow_id).
architecture_overview.approval_governance_flow: Mutating/critical agent actions do not execute immediately. ApprovalEngineService.create_approval writes an ApprovalRequest (status PENDING, execution_status WAITING_FOR_APPROVAL) + ApprovalEvent + AuditLog. A human hits POST /approvals/{id}/approve then POST /approvals/{id}/resume-workflow. resume_workflow looks up the executor keyed '{module}.{action}' in approval_agent/handlers.py::handler_registry, runs it, marks EXECUTED, and _resume_agent_run patches the originating AgentRun to COMPLETED with new events (e.g. EMPLOYEE_CREATED, LEAVE_APPROVED). Every transition writes AuditLog + ApprovalEvent.
architecture_overview.auth_flow: POST /auth/login (OAuth2 password form) -> AuthService.authenticate -> issue_tokens => access JWT (type 'access', 60 min) + refresh JWT (type 'refresh', 7 days; SHA-256 hash stored in refresh_tokens). Frontend keeps access token in memory (Zustand) and refresh token in sessionStorage. On any 401 the fetch client calls refreshSession() (POST /auth/refresh, which rotates+revokes) and retries once. RBAC: user.permissions (union of role permission codes, or ALL if is_superuser) gate both backend routes (require_permissions) and frontend routes/sidebar (hasPermission).

entrypoint.app: FastAPI(title=settings.project_name, version=settings.app_version, docs_url='/docs', redoc_url='/redoc', openapi_url='/api/v1/openapi.json', lifespan=lifespan)

conventions.base_model: backend/app/models/base.py: Base(DeclarativeBase); BaseModel(Base, __abstract__=True) gives EVERY table 5 columns: id (UUID PK default uuid4), tenant_id (UUID nullable, indexed), created_at (timestamptz server_default now()), updated_at (timestamptz server_default now(), onupdate now()), deleted_at (timestamptz nullable, indexed = soft-delete marker).
conventions.soft_delete: Records are soft-deleted by setting deleted_at = now(). Reads filter `deleted_at IS NULL`. Multi-tenant scaffolding exists (tenant_id) but is not yet enforced in queries.
conventions.rbac_dependencies: app/api/deps.py: get_current_user (decodes access JWT, loads User with roles+permissions, rejects inactive), require_permissions(*codes) (superuser bypass else set(codes) subset of user perms), require_roles(*names) (defined but currently unused by app/api endpoints).
conventions.audit_log_pattern: Mutating endpoints in employees/documents/masters capture current_user via Depends(get_current_user) and write an AuditLog(entity_type, entity_id, action, old_value, new_value, performed_by). Payroll endpoints DO NOT write AuditLog (known gap).
conventions.agent_registry_pattern: Domain agents subclass BaseAgent (app/agents/shared/base_agent.py) and are registered into the singleton agent_registry (app/agents/shared/agent_registry.py) by register_placeholder_agents() in coordinator_agent/service.py at import time. Approval executors register into handler_registry (approval_agent/handlers.py) keyed '{module}.{action}'.
conventions.schema_location: Cross-cutting schemas in app/schemas/{auth,common}.py; almost all feature schemas live in app/agents/<agent>/schemas.py and endpoint-local Pydantic classes inside the endpoint files.

functions.decode_token(token)->dict: jwt.decode; wraps JWTError into ValueError('Invalid token')

auth_middleware.behavior: dispatch sets request.state.user_id=None, parses 'Authorization: Bearer <token>', decodes it, and if type=='access' sets request.state.user_id=payload['sub']. Swallows ValueError. NEVER rejects a request — authorization is enforced by the deps, not here.

session.get_db: generator dependency, yields a Session, closes in finally

base_registry.role: Imports/re-exports every ORM model + Base so Alembic target_metadata=Base.metadata sees all tables. When you add a new model file you MUST import it here (or in a module this imports) or migrations/autogenerate will miss it.

models.note: 34 tables total. All inherit the 5 BaseModel columns (id, tenant_id, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at). Only the extra columns are listed below. Enums are Python StrEnum stored as String.

users.columns: email String(320) unique/idx NN; password_hash Text NN; first_name/last_name String(120) NN; is_active Bool=T; is_superuser Bool=F; last_login_at tz
users.relationships: roles (secondary user_roles, selectin); refresh_tokens (cascade delete-orphan); employee_profile (1:1 Employee)

roles.columns: name String(100) unique/idx NN; description Text

permissions.columns: code String(120) unique/idx NN; name String(160) NN; description Text

user_roles.columns: user_id FK users.id CASCADE NN; role_id FK roles.id CASCADE NN; UniqueConstraint(user_id,role_id)

role_permissions.columns: role_id FK roles.id CASCADE NN; permission_id FK permissions.id CASCADE NN; UniqueConstraint(role_id,permission_id)

refresh_tokens.columns: user_id FK users.id CASCADE NN; token_hash String(64) NN idx unique; expires_at tz NN; revoked_at tz

leave_types.columns: name String(120) NN (uq_leave_types_name); code String(40) NN idx; category String(40)=PAID; annual_allocation Numeric(8,2)=0; carry_forward_allowed Bool=F; requires_approval Bool=T; affects_payroll Bool=F; active Bool=T; is_paid Bool=T; annual_quota Numeric(8,2)=0; description Text

departments.columns: name String(160) NN unique; code String(60) idx; description Text; parent_department_id self-FK; active Bool=T idx

designations.columns: title String(160) NN unique; code String(60) idx; level String(80) idx; description Text

employees.columns: user_id FK users.id unique nullable; first_name/last_name String(120) nullable; employee_code String(80) unique; joining_date Date NN; employment_status String(40)=ACTIVE; employment_type String(40)=FULL_TIME idx; department_id FK; designation_id FK; reporting_manager_id self-FK; official_email String(320) unique; personal_email/phone idx; dob Date; gender String(30); emergency_contact JSONB; bank_account_number String(80); ifsc_code String(40); current_salary Numeric(14,2); pan_number idx; aadhaar_number; uan_number; profile_photo Text
employees.relationships: user, department, designation, reporting_manager(self), documents, assets, attendance_records, leave_requests, leave_balances, payroll_items

candidates.columns: first_name/last_name String(120); email idx; phone idx; source String(120) idx; resume_url Text; parsed_resume_json JSONB; current_company String(180); expected_ctc Numeric(14,2); notice_period String(80); candidate_status String(40)=NEW

resume_uploads.columns: candidate_id FK candidates.id; original_filename String(255) NN; stored_filename String(255) NN unique; storage_path Text NN; content_type String(120) NN; file_size Integer NN; uploaded_by FK users.id; parsed_text_preview Text; parsed_json JSONB

employee_documents.columns: employee_id FK employees.id CASCADE NN; document_type String(120) NN; document_url Text NN; status String(40)=PENDING; verified_by FK users.id; verified_at tz

employee_assets.columns: employee_id FK CASCADE NN; asset_type String(120) NN; asset_code String(120) NN idx; assigned_at/returned_at tz; asset_status String(40)=ASSIGNED; metadata_json JSONB

attendance_records.columns: employee_id FK CASCADE NN; attendance_date Date NN; check_in_at/check_out_at tz; check_in_time/check_out_time Time; status String(40) NN; total_hours Numeric(6,2); remarks Text; source String(80); metadata_json JSONB; UniqueConstraint(employee_id,attendance_date)

leave_requests.columns: employee_id FK CASCADE NN; leave_type_id FK leave_types.id nullable; leave_type String(80) NN (denormalized); from_date/to_date Date nullable; start_date/end_date Date NN; total_days Numeric(6,2) NN; reason Text; status String(40)=PENDING; requested_by/approved_by FK users.id; approved_at tz

leave_balances.columns: employee_id FK CASCADE NN; leave_type_id FK; leave_type String(80) NN; year Integer NN; allocated/opening_balance/accrued/used/remaining Numeric(8,2)=0; UniqueConstraints (employee_id,leave_type,year) and (employee_id,leave_type_id,year)

leave_approvals.columns: leave_request_id FK CASCADE NN; status String(40)=PENDING; approver_id FK users.id; comments Text; action_date tz

notifications.columns: user_id FK users.id CASCADE NN; title String(180) NN; message Text NN; channel String(60); status String(40)=UNREAD; payload_json JSONB

payroll_runs.columns: month Int NN; year Int NN; status String(40)=DRAFT; generated_by/approved_by FK users.id; approved_at tz; UniqueConstraint(month,year)

payroll_run_items.columns: payroll_run_id FK CASCADE NN; employee_id FK NN; gross_salary Numeric(14,2) NN; deductions Numeric(14,2)=0; lop_days Numeric(6,2)=0; net_salary Numeric(14,2) NN; bank_account_number String(80) NN; ifsc_code String(40) NN; UniqueConstraint(payroll_run_id,employee_id)

salary_components.columns: name String(120) NN unique; code String(50) NN idx unique; type String(40) NN (earning/deduction); calculation_type String(40) NN (fixed/percentage/formula); calculation_value Numeric(14,2); formula String(500); reference_component_code String(50); taxable Bool=T; active Bool=T

salary_structures.columns: name String(120) NN; code String(50) NN idx unique; description String(500); active Bool=T

salary_structure_items.columns: structure_id FK CASCADE NN; component_code String(50) NN; calculation_type String(40) NN; calculation_value Numeric(14,2); formula String(500); reference_component_code String(50); sort_order Int=0

employee_salary_assignments.columns: employee_id FK CASCADE NN; salary_structure_id FK NN; gross_salary Numeric(14,2) NN; effective_from Date NN; effective_to Date; status String(40)=PENDING_APPROVAL; approved_by FK users.id

salary_revision_history.columns: employee_id FK CASCADE NN; old_salary Numeric(14,2); new_salary Numeric(14,2) NN; revision_type String(40) NN; reason Text; effective_from Date NN; approved_by FK users.id

salary_assignment_approvals.columns: assignment_id FK CASCADE NN; status String(40)=PENDING; approver_id FK users.id; approved_at tz; comments Text

approval_requests.columns: module_name String(120) NN; action_name String(160) NN; payload_json JSONB; status String(40)=PENDING; workflow_id String(160); workflow_state_json JSONB; approval_reason Text; execution_status String(40)=WAITING_FOR_APPROVAL; requested_by/approved_by/rejected_by FK users.id; resumed_at/executed_at tz

approval_events.columns: approval_request_id FK CASCADE NN; event_type String(40) NN; message Text NN; payload_json JSONB; performed_by FK users.id

agent_runs.columns: agent_name String(160) NN; status String(40)=PENDING; started_at/completed_at tz; requested_by FK users.id; correlation_id String(120) idx (== public workflow_id); metadata_json JSONB (holds workflow_state, events, result)

agent_steps.columns: agent_run_id FK CASCADE NN; step_name String(180) NN; step_status String(40)=PENDING; input_json/output_json JSONB

audit_logs.columns: entity_type String(120) NN; entity_id UUID nullable; action String(120) NN; old_value/new_value JSONB; performed_by FK users.id

lookup_values.columns: category String(120) NN; code String(120) NN; label String(180) NN; sort_order Int=0; active Bool=T; metadata_json JSONB; UniqueConstraint(category,code)

api.mount: app/main.py mounts api_router (app/api/v1/router.py) at /api/v1. Router groups below list their mount prefix and source file. Full path = /api/v1 + prefix + route.

routes.auth: get_current_user ONLY (no permission gate — see known_issues)

payroll.note: salary component/structure master CRUD; gated by payroll:view but writes NO AuditLog

routes.errors: 409 if code change on component used by a structure

agents_and_agent_hosted_routers.note: These routers live INSIDE the agents package, not app/api/v1/endpoints. Detailed action lists are under backend.agents.

agent_command.note: frontend-friendly shape of the same CoordinatorRuntimeService

deps.get_current_user: decode access JWT -> AuthService.get_user_by_id -> reject if inactive/invalid (401 'Could not validate credentials')
deps.require_permissions(*codes): superuser bypass else set(codes) ⊆ user_permissions(user) else 403 'Insufficient permission'
deps.require_roles(*names): superuser bypass else role-name intersection else 403 'Insufficient role' — DEFINED BUT UNUSED by app/api endpoints

auth_service.py.class_AuthService: get_user_by_email/by_id (eager roles+permissions), authenticate (verify + update last_login_at), issue_tokens (access+refresh, persist hashed refresh), rotate_refresh_token (validate+revoke old+issue new), revoke_refresh_token
auth_service.py.user_permissions(user): ALL codes if superuser else union of role permission codes
auth_service.py.seed_auth_data(db): idempotent upsert of PERMISSIONS + ROLE_NAMES(+ROLE_PERMISSION_CODES) + admin superuser from settings

services.lop_calculator.py: LOPResult dataclass + calculate_lop(db, employee_id, month, year) — sums working/present/paid/unpaid/LOP days from AttendanceRecord + approved LeaveRequest; weekday fallback when no attendance
services.payroll_preparation_service.py: prepare_employee_payroll_input(...) wraps calculate_lop; prepare_monthly_payroll_input maps over many employees

shared_infra.base_agent.py: BaseAgent(ABC): attrs name/description/supported_actions/approval_required_actions/requires_approval; async execute(state), abstract run(state), async invoke(action,payload,context)
shared_infra.agent_registry.py: AgentRegistry singleton `agent_registry`: register/register_or_replace/get/list_agents/metadata()/async invoke — metadata() feeds GET /agents/registry
shared_infra.agent_events.py: AgentEventType StrEnum (WORKFLOW_CREATED, AGENT_STARTED/COMPLETED, APPROVAL_REQUIRED, WORKFLOW_PAUSED/RESUMED, EMPLOYEE_*, LEAVE_*, ATTENDANCE_*, LOP_CALCULATED, ...) + AgentEvent model
shared_infra.others: agent_state.py, state_store.py (WorkflowStateStore), memory.py, memory_manager.py, message_types.py (AgentMessage/AgentMessageType), runtime_context.py (RuntimeContext), extraction.py, exceptions.py

intent_layer.extract(message): runs _rule_extract; if openai_intent_enabled + openai_api_key set also runs _openai_extract and merges. Rule wins if rule_intent != ai_intent AND rule_conf >= 0.9 AND no missing fields (source 'rules_fallback'); else AI wins if ai_conf >= rule_conf or rule intent 'unknown'. On exception -> rules.
intent_layer.IntentExtraction: intent (IntentName literal ~37 intents), agent_name (AgentName literal), confidence 0-1, entities (IntentEntities: employee_name, manager_name, date_from/to, department, status, salary_amount, leave_type, payroll_month/year), missing_fields, clarification_question, canonical_command, source

coordinator.service.py: CoordinatorRuntimeService.submit_command is the REAL orchestrator (graph.py coordinator_graph is a thin skeleton). Flow described in architecture_overview.agent_command_flow.
coordinator.CRITICAL_ACTION_KEYWORDS: maps phrases ('create employee','update salary','approve leave','process payroll','onboard'...) -> (agent, action, approval_module, approval_action); these force an ApprovalRequest
coordinator.register_placeholder_agents(): runs at import; registers all domain agents + placeholders (offboarding_agent, notification_agent) into agent_registry

employee_agent.tools: tools.py (list/search/department_employees, get/find employee, update_employee_fields, create_employee_draft, soft_delete_employee, deactivate_employee, employee_profile)

payroll_agent.note: salary-component master agent; tools.py has normalize_code used by payroll endpoints

resume_parser_agent.flow: upload_and_parse validates+saves file, parser.py extracts text (pdfplumber/python-docx), creates Candidate+ResumeUpload

placeholders.notification_agent: actions inspect/send_official_email; approval send_official_email — both PlaceholderSpecializedAgent registered by coordinator

approvals.service.py_ApprovalEngineService: create_approval, list_pending, get_approval, approve, reject (runs '{module}.{action}.reject' handler if present), needs_changes, resume_workflow (runs '{module}.{action}' handler from handler_registry, marks EXECUTED, _resume_agent_run patches originating AgentRun). Every transition writes AuditLog + ApprovalEvent.
approvals.handlers.py: ApprovalHandlerRegistry singleton handler_registry + placeholder_handler. Pre-registered keys: employee.create/update/delete, payroll.process, payroll.generate_bank_sheet, leave.approve, offboarding.start. Domain agent handlers.py modules register the real executors (imported for side effects by coordinator service.py).
approvals.graph.py: approval_graph: pause_for_human (interrupt_manager.pause_for_approval) -> execute_after_approval (runs handler if APPROVED) -> END
approvals.api.py: REST endpoints serialize ApprovalRead with events + audit logs
approvals.note: The empty app/approvals/ and app/audit/ packages are scaffolding — real approval logic is here, real audit is the AuditLog model written inline in endpoints.

migrations.config: alembic.ini has a placeholder sqlalchemy.url; alembic/env.py OVERRIDES it with settings.database_url and sets target_metadata=Base.metadata (from app.db.base).
chain: 20260605_0015 leave_approvals_basemodel_indexes (NOW A NO-OP — see known_issues; originally re-added the same column/indexes and crashed upgrade)
migrations.how_to_add: cd backend; alembic revision -m 'desc' (or --autogenerate after ensuring the new model is imported via app/db/base.py); edit upgrade()/downgrade(); alembic upgrade head. Keep the linear single-head chain (down_revision = previous head). Prefer op.execute for data backfills.

entrypoint.tree: StrictMode > ThemeProvider (@/components/ui-system) > QueryClientProvider(new QueryClient()) > RouterProvider(router). Imports @/styles.css.

conventions.api_client: Frontend/src/services/api.ts — custom fetch wrapper (apiGet/apiPost/apiPatch/apiPut/apiDelete). Reads accessToken from useAuthStore. On 401 calls refreshSession() and retries once. Throws Error(payload.detail ?? 'API request failed: <status>'). BASE = import.meta.env.VITE_API_BASE_URL ?? 'http://127.0.0.1:8001/api/v1'.
conventions.server_state: TanStack Query; QueryClient created once in main.tsx; useQuery/useMutation declared inline in pages. Query keys: employees, employee-form-options, employee-detail, documents, leave-workspace, leave-policies, attendance-matrix, payroll-components, payroll-structures, masters, onboarding-workflows, health, lookups.
conventions.rbac: Route guard ProtectedRoute + Sidebar both key off the same *:view permission strings via useAuthStore.hasPermission.
conventions.imports: Design system re-exported from @/components/ui-system (barrel index.ts); UI primitives in @/components/ui.
conventions.styling: Tailwind with CSS-var HSL tokens defined in src/styles.css; darkMode 'class' toggled by ThemeProvider (localStorage 'hrms-theme').

routing.guard: Frontend/src/components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx — calls useAuthStore.initialize() on mount; shows LoadingSkeleton while idle/loading; redirects to /login if no user, to /unauthorized if permission set and !hasPermission; else <Outlet/>

pages.LoginPage.tsx: split-screen sign-in; useAuthStore.login; redirects to state.from or /dashboard
pages.DashboardPage.tsx: static StatCards + placeholders (no live queries yet)
pages.EmployeesPage.tsx: directory DataTable; useQuery(['employees'], getEmployees, refetch 15s); create wizard, profile/edit drawers, delete confirm (deleteEmployee)
pages.OnboardingPage.tsx: useQuery(['onboarding-workflows'], getWorkflows) filtered to onboarding agent; read-only
pages.AttendancePage.tsx: matrix/calendar; getAttendanceMatrix + getAttendanceDashboard; cell edit updateAttendanceCell
pages.LeavePage.tsx: getLeaveWorkspace; apply-leave form applyLeave; invalidates leave-workspace + attendance-matrix
pages.PayrollPage.tsx: salary component/structure config; getSalaryComponents/getSalaryStructures + getLookups([salary_component_type,salary_calculation_type]); CRUD components
pages.DocumentsPage.tsx: document register; getDocuments; add via createDocument + getLookups([document_type,document_status])
pages.AgentCommandPage.tsx: flagship conversational center (~1112 lines); submitAgentCommand, polls getWorkflows/getWorkflow, resume upload, renders BusinessResponseCards by StructuredResponse type
pages.PlaceholderPage.tsx: generic shell (title prop) for Candidates/Assets/Offboarding/Audit Logs/Settings

authStore.ts.actions: login(email,pw)->loginRequest+meRequest; logout()->logoutRequest; initialize()->silent refresh on load; refreshSession()->new access token or null; hasPermission(code)->is_superuser||permissions.includes

services.auth.ts: loginRequest POST /auth/login (x-www-form-urlencoded username/password), refreshRequest POST /auth/refresh, logoutRequest POST /auth/logout, meRequest GET /auth/me (uses its own fetch + API_BASE_URL)
services.employees.ts: getEmployees GET /employees?page_size=50; getEmployeeFormOptions GET /employees/form-options; createEmployee POST /employees; getEmployee GET /employees/{id}; updateEmployee PATCH /employees/{id}; deleteEmployee DELETE /employees/{id}
services.attendance.ts: getAttendanceMatrix GET /attendance/matrix; getAttendanceDashboard GET /attendance/dashboard; getEmployeeAttendanceSummary GET /attendance/employees/{id}/summary; updateAttendanceCell POST /attendance/actions
services.agents.ts: submitAgentCommand POST /agent-command/send; getWorkflows GET /agent-command/workflows; getWorkflow GET /agent-command/workflows/{id}; getOnboardingStateDebug GET /onboarding/state/{id}; getAgentRegistry GET /agents/registry; uploadOnboardingResume POST /resume/upload (XMLHttpRequest for progress)
services.masters.ts: getMasters GET /masters; createMaster POST /masters/{type}; updateMaster PATCH /masters/{type}/{id}; deleteMaster DELETE /masters/{type}/{id}

components.ui/: shadcn-style primitives: badge, button, card (Card/CardContent/CardHeader/CardTitle), input
components.ui-system/: design system + layout + feature cards, barrel index.ts. Layout: AppLayout (minimal prop for login), Sidebar (+ sidebarItems nav, permission-filtered), SidebarMenu, Header, Breadcrumbs, PageContainer, PageHeader, SectionCard. Data/UI: DataTable, StatCard, StatusBadge, Badge, FilterBar, SearchBar, Timeline, ActivityFeed, EmptyState, ErrorState, LoadingSkeleton, ToastNotification, ConfirmDialog, DrawerPanel, SheetPanel. Theming: theme-provider (ThemeProvider/useTheme). Agent/chat: ChatMessage, CommandInput, AgentStepCard, ApprovalCard.
components.BusinessResponseCards.tsx: ~1164-line feature-card library (EmployeePreviewCard, CandidateCard, PayrollSummaryCard, LeaveApprovalCard, OnboardingProgressCard, ApprovalDiffCard, AttendanceTable, EmployeeProfileDrawer, ...) exported via ui-system barrel; rendered by AgentCommandPage keyed off StructuredResponse type

types.note: most domain types are colocated in their service files

config.vite.config.ts: plugin-react; alias @ -> ./src; dev server.port 5173; build manualChunks react/motion/query
config.tailwind.config.ts: darkMode ['class']; content index.html + src/**/*.{ts,tsx}; HSL CSS-var tokens; custom borderRadius + boxShadow.soft
config.tsconfig: project refs tsconfig.app.json (strict, ES2022, moduleResolution Bundler, jsx react-jsx, @/* -> src/*) + tsconfig.node.json

permissions_catalog.note: 15 codes defined in backend/app/services/auth_service.py PERMISSIONS. Each backend route gate and frontend route/sidebar use the SAME code. Change both sides together.

codes.approvals:manage: granted to Super Admin/HR Admin (approval actions)

cross_cutting.auth_end_to_end: See architecture_overview.auth_flow. Backend: app/core/security.py + services/auth_service.py + api/deps.py + endpoints/auth.py. Frontend: services/auth.ts + services/api.ts + stores/authStore.ts + components/auth/ProtectedRoute.tsx.
known_issues_and_gotchas: MIGRATION FIXED: 20260605_0015 was crashing `alembic upgrade head` by re-adding leave_approvals.tenant_id + indexes already created by 0014. It is now a no-op. Do not re-add DDL there.
SEED SCRIPT: run `python -m scripts.seed_auth`, not `python scripts/seed_auth.py` (the latter fails ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'app').
FRONTEND FALLBACK PORT: services/api.ts and services/auth.ts default to :8001 when VITE_API_BASE_URL is unset, but the documented backend runs on :8000. Always set Frontend/.env.
AUTH GAP: GET /api/v1/lookups is gated by authentication only (get_current_user), NOT by a permission — any logged-in user can read all lookups.
AUDIT GAP: /api/v1/payroll/* endpoints mutate data but write NO AuditLog (unlike employees/documents/masters).
require_roles() exists in deps.py but no app/api endpoint uses it (only require_permissions + get_current_user).

LangGraph graphs for leave_agent and attendance_agent are placeholders (factory returns None); real logic runs in each *Agent.execute(). coordinator_graph is also a skeleton — CoordinatorRuntimeService.submit_command is the live orchestrator.
WorkflowState has no dedicated table — it is serialized into agent_runs.metadata_json; agent_runs.correlation_id IS the public workflow_id.
Two migration files share the 20260601_0011 filename prefix but have distinct revision ids (…0011 and …0012).
config.access_token_expire_minutes has no env alias, so ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES in .env is not actually read (stays 60).

add_backend_endpoint: 1. Create/extend a handler module in backend/app/api/v1/endpoints/<feature>.py with `router = APIRouter()`; define request/response Pydantic models locally or in app/agents/<x>/schemas.py.

2. Gate mutations with dependencies=[Depends(require_permissions('<code>'))] from app/api/deps.py; capture the actor with current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user) when you need to write AuditLog.
3. Use db: Session = Depends(get_db). Filter reads with deleted_at IS NULL; soft-delete by setting deleted_at.
4. Register the router in backend/app/api/v1/router.py: api_router.include_router(<feature>.router, prefix='/<feature>', tags=['<feature>']).
5. If it mutates business data, write AuditLog(entity_type,entity_id,action,old_value,new_value,performed_by) like employees.py does.
6. Update this doc: backend.api.groups.<feature> and cross_cutting.permissions_catalog.
add_model_and_migration: 1. Add the SQLAlchemy class in the right file under backend/app/models/<domain>/models.py, inheriting BaseModel (gives id/tenant_id/created_at/updated_at/deleted_at).
2. Ensure it is imported by backend/app/db/base.py (add to imports/__all__) so Alembic autogenerate + create_all see it.
3. cd backend; alembic revision -m '<desc>' (or --autogenerate). Set down_revision to the current head (20260612_0024 or later). Keep a single linear head.
4. Fill upgrade()/downgrade(); use op.execute for data backfills; do NOT re-create columns another migration already made (see the 0014/0015 gotcha).
6. Add Pydantic schemas + endpoint + frontend service as needed.
7. Update this doc: backend.models.<domain>, backend.migrations.chain, backend.migrations.current_head.
add_new_agent: 1. Create backend/app/agents/<name>_agent/ with service.py subclassing BaseAgent (set name, supported_actions, approval_required_actions).
2. If it needs an HTTP surface, add api.py with `router = APIRouter()` and register it in app/api/v1/router.py.
3. Register the agent into agent_registry — add it to register_placeholder_agents() in coordinator_agent/service.py (imported at startup).

4. For approval-gated actions, register executors in <name>_agent/handlers.py keyed '{module}.{action}' into handler_registry, and import that module for side effects in coordinator service.py; add keyword mappings to CRITICAL_ACTION_KEYWORDS if the coordinator should route to it.
5. Add intent names/routing in shared/natural_language.py (IntentName/AgentName) and coordinator routing if it should be reachable via /agent-command.
6. Update this doc: backend.agents.domain_agents.<name>_agent and backend.api if a router was added.
add_agent_action: 1. Add the action string to the agent's supported_actions and implement it in the agent's run/execute dispatch.
2. If mutating/critical: add to approval_required_actions and register a '{module}.{action}' handler in handlers.py.
3. Add/extend intent mapping in natural_language.py so the coordinator can route to it.
add_frontend_page: 1. Create Frontend/src/pages/<X>Page.tsx; use ui-system components (AppLayout is applied at layout level).
2. Add a route in Frontend/src/routes/router.tsx wrapped in <ProtectedRoute permission='<code>' />.
3. Add the nav entry to sidebarItems in components/ui-system/Sidebar.tsx (permission-filtered).
4. Fetch data with useQuery/useMutation using a service from services/ (add one if missing).
5. Update this doc: frontend.routing.routes, frontend.pages, and cross_cutting.permissions_catalog.
wire_frontend_to_new_endpoint: 1. Add a function in Frontend/src/services/<feature>.ts using apiGet/apiPost/apiPatch/apiPut/apiDelete from services/api.ts (path is relative to /api/v1; the client prepends VITE_API_BASE_URL).
2. Define the TS response type in the same service file (that is the repo convention).
3. Consume via useQuery/useMutation in the page; pick a stable query key and invalidate related keys after mutations.
debug_backend_500: Reproduce against http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs or curl; read the uvicorn traceback.

Common causes here: DB column/enum mismatch vs a model (check backend.models vs actual table with \d <table>); missing migration (alembic current vs head 20260612_0024); a service raising (services/*.py); an agent handler throwing during resume_workflow.
Trace: endpoint (backend.api.groups.*) -> service/agent tool -> model. Use the file paths in this doc to open the exact function.
debug_auth_401: 401 'Could not validate credentials' comes from api/deps.get_current_user: token missing/expired/type!='access', or user inactive/not found.

Frontend auto-retries once via refreshSession() (services/api.ts). If refresh also fails the user is logged out. Check refresh_tokens row (revoked_at/expires_at) and that JWT_SECRET_KEY matches the one that signed the token.
403 'Insufficient permission' = require_permissions gate; verify the user's roles/permissions (cross_cutting.permissions_catalog) or use the superuser admin.
debug_migration_failure: Read the alembic traceback. DuplicateColumn/DuplicateTable => a later migration re-does DDL an earlier one already applied (the 0014/0015 pattern) — make the later one a no-op or guard it.
'Can't locate revision' => broken chain (down_revision points at a missing id) — fix the revision links; keep one linear head.
Wrong DB => remember env.py uses settings.database_url (from backend/.env), not alembic.ini.
Recover: fix the offending version file, then `alembic upgrade head` (it resumes from alembic_version).
trace_an_agent_command_end_to_end: Approval (if critical): ApprovalEngineService.create_approval -> ApprovalRequest; workflow WAITING_APPROVAL.
State: persisted on agent_runs.metadata_json keyed by correlation_id; poll GET /agent-command/workflows/{id} + /events.
Resume: POST /approvals/{id}/approve then /resume-workflow -> handler_registry['{module}.{action}'] -> _resume_agent_run patches the AgentRun to COMPLETED.

update_protocol.principle: This doc must be edited in the SAME change as the code it describes, so it never drifts.

backend/app/models/**: if a table added: backend.db.base_registry note

always_also: Bump $meta.last_synced_git_commit to the new commit hash
Add any newly discovered footgun to cross_cutting.known_issues_and_gotchas
update_protocol.paste_this_instruction_to_your_ai_tool: You are editing the Agentic HRMS repo. docs/architecture.json is the authoritative map of the code. (1) Before writing code, read the relevant section and the matching entry under `playbooks`; only touch the files it names and copy the shown pattern. (2) After writing code, update docs/architecture.json per its `update_protocol.when_you_change_code_update_these_json_paths`, and bump $meta.last_synced_git_commit. (3) Never invent paths/tables/routes/permissions not present in this file — if something is missing, say so. Cite file:line in your explanation.
